events.thought: Bootstrap the system: start with core data models (users, tenants, subscriptions), basic auth middleware, and webhook infrastructure. These are blocking dependencies for everything else.

assignments.spec: Define Sequelize models: User (email, password hash, createdAt), Tenant (name, stripeId, ownerId FK), Subscription (tenantId FK, stripeId, plan, status, currentPeriodEnd), Invoice (tenantId FK, amount, status, lineItems JSON). Add timestamps & indexes.
JWT-based auth middleware: verify tokens, attach user/tenant to req. Include signup/login routes with bcrypt password hashing, token generation, and tenant auto-creation on signup.
Stripe webhook handler for invoice.created, customer.subscription.updated, charge.succeeded. Parse events, validate signature, update Invoice & Subscription models in DB. Export router.
React admin dashboard: fetch & display list of tenants (name, stripeId, subscription status, invoice count) in a table. Add sidebar nav. Use React Query for data fetching, Tailwind for styling.

events.spec: Define Sequelize models: User (email, password hash, createdAt), Tenant (name, stripeId, ownerId FK), Subscription (tenantId FK, stripeId, plan, status, currentPeriodEnd), Invoice (tenantId FK, amount, status, lineItems JSON). Add timestamps & indexes.
JWT-based auth middleware: verify tokens, attach user/tenant to req. Include signup/login routes with bcrypt password hashing, token generation, and tenant auto-creation on signup.
Stripe webhook handler for invoice.created, customer.subscription.updated, charge.succeeded. Parse events, validate signature, update Invoice & Subscription models in DB. Export router.
React admin dashboard: fetch & display list of tenants (name, stripeId, subscription status, invoice count) in a table. Add sidebar nav. Use React Query for data fetching, Tailwind for styling.

events.thought: Turn 2: Build core billing logic (subscriptions, invoices), add Stripe integration helpers, create auth routes, and write tests for auth flow.

assignments.spec: Express routes: POST /auth/register (email, password), POST /auth/login (return JWT), POST /auth/logout. Hash passwords with bcrypt.
Service layer: createSubscription(tenantId, priceId, seatCount), listSubscriptions(tenantId), cancelSubscription(subscriptionId). Interact with Stripe SDK.
Stripe helpers: initializeCustomer(tenantId, email), createSubscription(customerId, priceId), createInvoice(customerId, items). Wrap Stripe SDK calls.
Jest + supertest: test POST /auth/register success/duplicate email, POST /auth/login success/invalid credentials, JWT token verification.

events.spec: Express routes: POST /auth/register (email, password), POST /auth/login (return JWT), POST /auth/logout. Hash passwords with bcrypt.
Service layer: createSubscription(tenantId, priceId, seatCount), listSubscriptions(tenantId), cancelSubscription(subscriptionId). Interact with Stripe SDK.
Stripe helpers: initializeCustomer(tenantId, email), createSubscription(customerId, priceId), createInvoice(customerId, items). Wrap Stripe SDK calls.
Jest + supertest: test POST /auth/register success/duplicate email, POST /auth/login success/invalid credentials, JWT token verification.

events.thought: We have auth, subscription service, and Stripe integration sketched. Now we need: (1) invoice generation + line items, (2) database schema/migrations, (3) tenant routes, (4) webhook tests. Let's build the invoice layer and flesh out routes.

assignments.spec: Service layer: generateInvoice(tenantId, subscriptionId) creates invoice record with line items (seat count × price or usage total), stores in DB, returns invoice object.
Express routes: GET /tenants (list all), POST /tenants (create org with name), GET /tenants/:id (detail + subscriptions), requires auth middleware.
Process Stripe webhooks: handle payment_intent.succeeded → update invoice status, customer.subscription.deleted → mark sub inactive, log all events.
Jest: test generateInvoice with valid subscriptionId, verify line items created, test with missing tenant/sub (error cases).

events.spec: Service layer: generateInvoice(tenantId, subscriptionId) creates invoice record with line items (seat count × price or usage total), stores in DB, returns invoice object.
Express routes: GET /tenants (list all), POST /tenants (create org with name), GET /tenants/:id (detail + subscriptions), requires auth middleware.
Process Stripe webhooks: handle payment_intent.succeeded → update invoice status, customer.subscription.deleted → mark sub inactive, log all events.
Jest: test generateInvoice with valid subscriptionId, verify line items created, test with missing tenant/sub (error cases).

events.thought: We have auth, subscriptions, invoices, and Stripe webhooks. Now need: (1) database schema + migrations, (2) subscription routes, (3) invoice routes, (4) React admin dashboard scaffold, (5) integration tests for webhook flow. Start with DB schema so all services have a foundation.

assignments.spec: PostgreSQL schema: users (id, email, password_hash, created_at), tenants (id, name, stripe_customer_id, owner_id), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, price_id, seat_count, status, current_period_start/end), invoices (id, tenant_id, subscription_id, amount, status, issued_at, due_at, line_items JSON). Include indexes on tenant_id, user_id, stripe_subscription_id.

Express routes: GET /subscriptions (list by tenant), POST /subscriptions (create via stripeService), PATCH /subscriptions/:id (update seat_count or status), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel). All routes require tenantId middleware.
Express routes: GET /invoices (list by tenant), GET /invoices/:id (fetch single invoice + line_items), POST /invoices/:id/retry (retry payment via Stripe). All require tenantId + auth.

React component using Tailwind: page layout with nav sidebar, main content area. Show list of tenants (table with name, stripe_customer_id, created_at, actions). Include search + pagination. Fetch GET /api/tenants on mount. Export default Dashboard.

events.spec: PostgreSQL schema: users (id, email, password_hash, created_at), tenants (id, name, stripe_customer_id, owner_id), subscriptions (id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, price_id, seat_count, status, current_period_start/end), invoices (id, tenant_id, subscription_id, amount, status, issued_at, due_at, line_items JSON). Include indexes on tenant_id, user_id, stripe_subscription_id.

Express routes: GET /subscriptions (list by tenant), POST /subscriptions (create via stripeService), PATCH /subscriptions/:id (update seat_count or status), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel). All routes require tenantId middleware.
Express routes: GET /invoices (list by tenant), GET /invoices/:id (fetch single invoice + line_items), POST /invoices/:id/retry (retry payment via Stripe). All require tenantId + auth.

React component using Tailwind: page layout with nav sidebar, main content area. Show list of tenants (table with name, stripe_customer_id, created_at, actions). Include search + pagination. Fetch GET /api/tenants on mount. Export default Dashboard.

events.thought: We have routes and services, but missing: auth middleware, subscription service layer, Stripe client setup, and webhook route. Let's build auth, subscription logic, Stripe integration plumbing, and webhook endpoint.

assignments.spec: JWT middleware: verifyToken(req, res, next) extracts Bearer token, validates signature, attaches userId + tenantId to req.user. Also export signToken(userId, tenantId, secret).
Service layer: createSubscription(tenantId, planType, seatCount), getSubscriptionsByTenant(tenantId), updateSubscriptionSeats(subscriptionId, newCount). Support per-seat pricing tiers.
Initialize Stripe SDK with API key from env, export stripeClient. Helper functions: createStripeCustomer(tenantId, email), syncSubscriptionToStripe(subscriptionId, planId, quantity).
Express route POST /webhooks/stripe that validates Stripe signature, calls stripeWebhookService handlers for payment_intent.succeeded and invoice.payment_succeeded events.

events.spec: JWT middleware: verifyToken(req, res, next) extracts Bearer token, validates signature, attaches userId + tenantId to req.user. Also export signToken(userId, tenantId, secret).

Initialize Stripe SDK with API key from env, export stripeClient. Helper functions: createStripeCustomer(tenantId, email), syncSubscriptionToStripe(subscriptionId, planId, quantity).
Express route POST /webhooks/stripe that validates Stripe signature, calls stripeWebhookService handlers for payment_intent.succeeded and invoice.payment_succeeded events.

events.thought: We have auth, subscriptions, invoices, and webhook skeleton. Need: (1) user auth endpoints, (2) tenant CRUD, (3) invoice service logic, (4) webhook handler implementation, (5) frontend for tenants/subs/invoices, (6) tests on critical paths. Start with auth endpoints + tenant routes, then flesh out invoice service + webhook handlers.

assignments.spec: POST /auth/register (email, password, create user + tenant), POST /auth/login (email, password, return JWT token)
GET /tenants (list for current user), POST /tenants (create new org), GET /tenants/:id (by user), PUT /tenants/:id (update name/metadata)
Handle invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.updated webhooks; update invoice + subscription status in DB
Jest + supertest: test register success/duplicate email, login success/wrong password, JWT token issued and verified

Handle invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.updated webhooks; update invoice + subscription status in DB
Jest + supertest: test register success/duplicate email, login success/wrong password, JWT token issued and verified

events.thought: We have auth, tenants, subscriptions foundation, and webhook handling. Need: (1) subscriptions API routes, (2) invoices + line items DB models/routes, (3) frontend dashboard skeleton, (4) critical path tests. Let's build subscriptions routes, invoice models, dashboard, and subscription tests.

assignments.spec: Express routes: GET /subscriptions (list by tenant), POST /subscriptions (create via subscriptionService), PATCH /subscriptions/:id (update seat count or plan), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel). All require auth middleware.
Prisma schema additions: Invoice (id, tenantId, subscriptionId, status, totalAmount, dueDate, paidDate, createdAt) + InvoiceLineItem (id, invoiceId, description, amount, quantity). Include relations.
React dashboard: fetch & display list of tenants (card layout), subscriptions per tenant (table: plan, seats, status), invoices (payment status). Use Tailwind. Add logout button.
Jest + supertest: test POST /subscriptions (create per-seat), GET /subscriptions (list), PATCH (update seats), verify 401 unauthorized, verify tenantId isolation.

events.spec: Express routes: GET /subscriptions (list by tenant), POST /subscriptions (create via subscriptionService), PATCH /subscriptions/:id (update seat count or plan), DELETE /subscriptions/:id (cancel). All require auth middleware.
Prisma schema additions: Invoice (id, tenantId, subscriptionId, status, totalAmount, dueDate, paidDate, createdAt) + InvoiceLineItem (id, invoiceId, description, amount, quantity). Include relations.
React dashboard: fetch & display list of tenants (card layout), subscriptions per tenant (table: plan, seats, status), invoices (payment status). Use Tailwind. Add logout button.
Jest + supertest: test POST /subscriptions (create per-seat), GET /subscriptions (list), PATCH (update seats), verify 401 unauthorized, verify tenantId isolation.

events.thought: We have auth, tenants, subscriptions, and a basic dashboard. Now we need: invoice routes + line items, Stripe product/price sync, webhook verification middleware, and dashboard subscription/invoice views. Let's build invoice endpoints, sync Stripe products, add webhook security, and extend the dashboard.

assignments.spec: Express routes: GET /invoices (list by tenant), POST /invoices/manual (create invoice with line items for a subscription), GET /invoices/:id (single invoice detail). All require tenant auth.
Functions to sync Stripe products/prices to local DB on startup and via admin endpoint. Create/update Product and Price records. Handle currency and billing intervals.
Middleware to verify Stripe webhook signature using raw body + webhook secret. Attach verified payload to req.body for handler.
React page: show single tenant, list subscriptions + status, list recent invoices with download links, create subscription button. Use React Router.

events.spec: Express routes: GET /invoices (list by tenant), POST /invoices/manual (create invoice with line items for a subscription), GET /invoices/:id (single invoice detail). All require tenant auth.
Functions to sync Stripe products/prices to local DB on startup and via admin endpoint. Create/update Product and Price records. Handle currency and billing intervals.
Middleware to verify Stripe webhook signature using raw body + webhook secret. Attach verified payload to req.body for handler.
React page: show single tenant, list subscriptions + status, list recent invoices with download links, create subscription button. Use React Router.

events.thought: We have core backend routes, Stripe sync, and basic UI. Now need: (1) webhook handler for invoice.payment_succeeded/failed, (2) subscription status updates from Stripe, (3) invoice line items display, (4) critical path tests for auth + billing flow.

assignments.spec: Express webhook route POST /webhooks/stripe that verifies signature and dispatches to handlers for invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.updated. Update invoice status + send email notifications.
Service to sync subscription status from Stripe (active/canceled/past_due), handle auto-renewal logic, and mark invoices paid/failed based on webhook events.
React page showing single subscription details: plan name, seats/usage, renewal date, status badge (active/past_due/canceled), list of invoices with line items and payment status.
End-to-end Jest test: register user → create tenant → create subscription → verify invoice created → simulate Stripe webhook → verify invoice marked paid. Cover happy path + failed payment.

events.spec: Express webhook route POST /webhooks/stripe that verifies signature and dispatches to handlers for invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.updated. Update invoice status + send email notifications.
Service to sync subscription status from Stripe (active/canceled/past_due), handle auto-renewal logic, and mark invoices paid/failed based on webhook events.
React page showing single subscription details: plan name, seats/usage, renewal date, status badge (active/past_due/canceled), list of invoices with line items and payment status.
End-to-end Jest test: register user → create tenant → create subscription → verify invoice created → simulate Stripe webhook → verify invoice marked paid. Cover happy path + failed payment.

events.thought: System is nearly complete. Need: (1) invoice list/detail UI pages, (2) dashboard home page, (3) critical path tests for subscriptions & webhooks, (4) database migrations/seed script. Let's add invoice UI, subscription tests, and migrations.

assignments.spec: React page: list invoices for current tenant, show status/amount/date, link to detail. Filter by subscription.
React admin dashboard home: summary cards (total tenants, active subscriptions, MRR), recent invoices table, navigation menu.
Jest test: create subscription via API, mock Stripe webhook (customer.subscription.created), verify DB sync, check invoice generation.
PostgreSQL schema: users, tenants, subscriptions, invoices, line_items tables with proper FKs, indexes, timestamps.

events.spec: React page: list invoices for current tenant, show status/amount/date, link to detail. Filter by subscription.

PostgreSQL schema: users, tenants, subscriptions, invoices, line_items tables with proper FKs, indexes, timestamps.

events.thought: System is nearly complete. Need: (1) invoice creation service to generate line items when subscriptions change, (2) billing cycle + charge logic, (3) Stripe customer sync, (4) final integration test covering full webhook→invoice flow, (5) env config template. Let's prioritize the invoice service + charging logic, then webhook→invoice integration test.

assignments.spec: Service to generate invoices: createInvoiceFromSubscription(subscriptionId, periodStart, periodEnd) creates invoice + line items, calculates amount due based on plan pricing

Service to sync Stripe customer + payment method data: syncStripeCustomer(tenantId) creates/updates Stripe customer, stores customer_id in DB; chargeCustomer(invoiceId) creates Stripe charge from invoice amount
React admin page: list all tenants with filters (status, created date), show tenant ID/email/subscription count, link to view/edit tenant details
End-to-end Jest test: subscribe user → mock Stripe webhook (invoice.payment_succeeded) → verify invoice created in DB with correct line items + status updated

events.spec: Service to generate invoices: createInvoiceFromSubscription(subscriptionId, periodStart, periodEnd) creates invoice + line items, calculates amount due based on plan pricing

Service to sync Stripe customer + payment method data: syncStripeCustomer(tenantId) creates/updates Stripe customer, stores customer_id in DB; chargeCustomer(invoiceId) creates Stripe charge from invoice amount
React admin page: list all tenants with filters (status, created date), show tenant ID/email/subscription count, link to view/edit tenant details
End-to-end Jest test: subscribe user → mock Stripe webhook (invoice.payment_succeeded) → verify invoice created in DB with correct line items + status updated

events.thought: System is near complete. Need: (1) webhook endpoint to handle Stripe events, (2) subscription service to manage subscription lifecycle, (3) auth middleware, (4) smoke tests for critical happy paths. Then ship.

assignments.spec: JWT/session auth middleware: verifyToken(), requireAuth(), extractTenantId(). Protect all non-public routes.
Express POST /webhooks/stripe endpoint: validate Stripe signature, handle customer.subscription.updated/deleted events, call invoiceManager + stripeSync.
Service: createSubscription(tenantId, priceId, quantity), updateSubscription(), cancelSubscription(). Sync with Stripe API.
Jest smoke test: user signup → create tenant → create subscription → receive webhook → invoice generated → verify DB state.
